content: Are the bindings docs intentionally hidden from https://rust-for-linux.github.io/docs, or can I just not find them?
bindings varies from different configurations, how would you suggest the documentation would be like?

That's a good point, I'm not sure. I don't think it would hurt to also publish the bindings for core modules like fs and mm with the same configuration that the kernel docs build with, just to have a quick reference for what the Rust interfaces look like
Note that rust/bindings/bindings_generated.rs is a single file with only one module.
So it requires some effort to find "bindings for core modules like fs and mm".

Yeah, selective filtering would be yucky (I think there's an open request for bindgen to allow by-module splitting like this somehow). Would it not be suitable to just generate the bindings with a default .config plus Rust enables? I assumed that is what the Rust documentation are doing anyway

I think it could be reasonable if the docs for bindings/lib.rs just clearly state that it shouldn't be trusted in all configurations and is just for reference, which I guess is already true even on the Rust side. Or use the same config that https://docs.kernel.org/core-api/index.html uses (I feel like even some C users would wind up using the rustdoc output since it's so much more searchable and better crosslinked than sphinx)
Or even write the entire .config to pub mod config { #[doc = include_file!(".config") ] } so there's a reference (not sure if there's a way to get overrides)

Well, it's not a bad idea. But putting something like that in a public website always require some justification ;-)

I think one way to proceed is to add a rust-binding-doc(or other names) make target for kernel, so that it's easier for people to generate binding doc for themselves at first. Care to try that first? ;-) ;-) ;-)
That sounds like a good idea, I will try that out sometime soon

I posted something for this here: https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20230803093418.51872-1-tmgross@umich.edu/T/#t. I didn't add a separate target yet since I wasn't sure if we'd prefer a target or config (will follow up on the list)
